subject: Physics
title: Three Mile Island
description: A short lesson to help you apply Three Mile Island.

hook.text: In 1979, a partial meltdown at Three Mile Island in Pennsylvania terrified America—and barely any radiation escaped.

buildup.text: A stuck valve caused coolant to drain from the reactor. Warning lights confused the operators.

discovery.text: The fuel partially melted, but the containment building held. Radiation released was minimal.

twist.text: The film 'The China Syndrome' about a nuclear meltdown had opened in theaters just 12 days earlier.

climax.text: No injuries occurred, but the incident halted new nuclear plant construction in the US for 30 years.

punchline.text: Minimal harm. Maximum fear. Nuclear power's reputation never recovered.

quiz.question: What was the outcome of the Three Mile Island accident?
options: Minimal radiation released but it halted US nuclear construction for decades
Thousands were exposed to dangerous radiation
